repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-AMG GLA45 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth it on any well-maintained GLA45
Replacement often £5,500–£8,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Mercedes-AMG GLA45? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 facelift (381PS M133 upgraded) — more power and revised components
* Documented turbocharger history — oil feed line recall completed
* Documented timing chain history on higher-mileage examples
* Full service history with regular oil changes (more frequent than standard is better)
avoidIf: * Any whistling or power loss — turbocharger failure risk
* Cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is serious
* Transmission overheating warnings — DCT issues can be expensive
* Poor service history — the M133 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The GLA45 is a unique proposition — a compact SUV with genuine AMG firepower. The M133 is a remarkable engine, winning multiple International Engine of the Year awards, but it's a high-strung performance unit that demands respect. Turbocharger stress and timing chain stretch are the defining concerns. A post-2015 facelift car with full history is the buy. Maintain the turbo, timing chain and gearbox, and the GLA45 will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-AMG GLA45 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain stretch warning)
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any whistling, hesitation, or loss of power?
* Service History — Check for turbo oil feed line recall completion
* Oil Leaks — Any oil smell or spots? Valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets
* Transmission Behaviour — Any hard shifts, overheating, or clunks? (7G-DCT)
* Carbon Build-up — Any misfires or flat spots? (walnut blasting history?)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine (M133.980) typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. A turbocharger replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

items.question: Is the Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine reliable?
items.answer: The M133 is generally durable but can develop issues as mileage rises. Turbocharger stress and timing chain stretch are the defining concerns. The 2015+ facelift upgraded output from 360PS to 381PS. Proper maintenance is critical — regular oil changes (more frequent than the standard schedule) and proactive attention to the turbo and timing chain are essential.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-AMG GLA45?
items.answer: Turbocharger stress/failure (power loss, whistling, £1,500–£3,500), timing chain stretch (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500), carbon build-up on intake valves (misfires, flat spots, £400–£800), oil leaks (valve cover/oil filter housing, £300–£800), and 7G-DCT overheating (transmission warnings, £800–£2,500).

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: GLA45 values range from £12,000–£35,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £5,500–£8,500 reconditioned engine represents 15–70% of vehicle value — our 70% Mercedes-Benz scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes-AMG GLA45 engine code?
items.answer: The GLA45 uses the M133.980 engine code — Mercedes-AMG's 2.0-litre turbocharged inline-four. Pre-2015 models produce 360PS; 2015+ models produce 381PS. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the timing chain stretch issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£5,500–£8,500). There are cases of premature chain stretching, especially with infrequent oil changes or aggressive operation. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic engine failure.
